method_atom: a model of many interacting units governed by the simplest pairwise coupling rule, in which local interactions drive the units to aggregate into clusters whose resulting size distribution self-organizes into a scale-free power-law (rank-size) statistic
object_atom: human individuals aggregating into cities, whose population sizes obey Zipf's law of city-size distribution
brief_reason: the pairwise-interaction mechanism that generates a power-law cluster-size distribution is the transferable method, while people clustering into cities is the domain-specific object.
